title: OOF pit-window hazard priors
summary: Pit decisions are closer to discrete-time hazard modeling than generic classification: probability rises at compound/stint-specific tyre ages and race-progress windows.
rationale: Pit decisions are closer to discrete-time hazard modeling than generic classification: probability rises at compound/stint-specific tyre ages and race-progress windows. Prior summaries mentioned hazard framing, but the testable step is explicit out-of-fold empirical priors for pit rate by Compound, Stint, TyreLife bins, RaceProgress bins, and Race/Year when safe.
implementation_hint: Create smoothed OOF target encodings such as pit_rate_by_compound_stint_tyrelife, pit_rate_by_stint_progress_bin, and cumulative historical hazard within each stint. Use fold-only statistics for train and full-train statistics for test; blend with global prior for rare bins.
expected_effect: Should sharpen ranking around typical stop windows and complement tree interactions already present.
risk: High leakage risk if encodings are computed on all train rows before validation; rare race-specific bins can overfit.
